ucs.title: [LLM] MuddyWater (MERCURY / Mango Sandstorm) RMM-tool initial access via spearphishing-attached installer
ucs.description: Hunts MuddyWater's signature 2022-2024 intrusion pattern: spearphish delivers a PDF/HTML pointing to a ConnectWise Control (ScreenConnect), Atera, SimpleHelp, RemoteUtilities or Syncro MSI/EXE that drops in Downloads/Temp and is launched by a browser, mail client, or msiexec. CISA AA22-055A and Microsoft's Mango Sandstorm reporting both pin this exact tooling — it's the cluster's most reliable foothold tradecraft.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as file_path values(Filesystem.process_name) as parent_process from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action IN ("created","renamed") (Filesystem.file_name IN ("ScreenConnect.ClientSetup.msi","ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe","ScreenConnect.WindowsClient.exe","AteraAgent.msi","AteraAgent.exe","setup_atera.msi","rutserv.exe","rfusclient.exe","RemoteUtilitiesViewer.exe","SimpleHelp.exe","Remote Access.exe","SyncroLive.Agent.Installer.exe","Syncro.Installer.exe","AnyDesk.exe") OR Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\ScreenConnect*","*\\AteraAgent*","*\\Remote Utilities*","*\\SimpleHelp*","*\\Syncro*")) (Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\Downloads\\*","*\\Temp\\*","*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*","*\\Users\\Public\\*")) by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("outlook.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","winrar.exe","7zg.exe","explorer.exe","WINWORD.EXE","EXCEL.EXE") (Processes.process_name="msiexec.exe" OR Processes.process IN ("*ScreenConnect*","*AteraAgent*","*RemoteUtilities*","*SimpleHelp*","*Syncro*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename _time as exec_time] | where exec_time>=firstTime AND exec_time<=lastTime+1800 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.kql:
let RmmFileNames = dynamic(["screenconnect.clientsetup.msi","screenconnect.clientservice.exe","screenconnect.windowsclient.exe","ateraagent.msi","ateraagent.exe","setup_atera.msi","rutserv.exe","rfusclient.exe","remoteutilitiesviewer.exe","simplehelp.exe","remote access.exe","syncrolive.agent.installer.exe","syncro.installer.exe","anydesk.exe"]);
let RmmFolderTokens = dynamic([@"\ScreenConnect",@"\Atera",@"\Remote Utilities",@"\SimpleHelp",@"\Syncro",@"\AnyDesk"]);
let DropZones = dynamic([@"\Downloads\",@"\Temp\",@"\AppData\Local\Temp\",@"\Users\Public\"]);
let BrowserOrMail = dynamic(["outlook.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","winrar.exe","7zg.exe","explorer.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe"]);
let Drops = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed")
    | where (tolower(FileName) in (RmmFileNames)) or FolderPath has_any (RmmFolderTokens)
    | where FolderPath has_any (DropZones)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (BrowserOrMail) or InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "msiexec.exe"
    | project DropTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, FileName, FolderPath,
              DropParent = InitiatingProcessFileName, AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (BrowserOrMail) or InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "msiexec.exe"
| where (FileName =~ "msiexec.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("ScreenConnect","Atera","RemoteUtilities","SimpleHelp","Syncro"))
     or FolderPath has_any (RmmFolderTokens)
     or tolower(FileName) in (RmmFileNames)
| join kind=inner Drops on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (DropTime .. DropTime + 30m)
| project DropTime, ExecTime = Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          DroppedFile = strcat(FolderPath, "\\", FileName),
          DropParent, ChildImage = FolderPath, ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine, SHA256
| order by ExecTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let RmmFileTokens = dynamic(["ScreenConnect","AteraAgent","Atera Networks","Remote Utilities","rutserv.exe","rfusclient.exe","SimpleHelp","Syncro","SyncroLive","AnyDesk"]);
let BrowserOrMail = dynamic(["outlook.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","winrar.exe","7zg.exe","explorer.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe"]);
let Drops = ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed")
    | where TargetFileName has_any (RmmFileTokens) or TargetFilePath has_any (RmmFileTokens)
    | where TargetFilePath has_any (@"\Downloads\", @"\Temp\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Users\Public\")
    | where ActingProcessName has_any (BrowserOrMail) or ActingProcessName has "msiexec.exe"
    | project DropTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, TargetFilePath, TargetFileName,
              DropParent = ActingProcessName, ActorUsername;
ImProcessCreate
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ParentProcessName has_any (BrowserOrMail) or ParentProcessName has "msiexec.exe"
| where TargetProcessCommandLine has_any (RmmFileTokens) or TargetProcessName has_any (RmmFileTokens)
| join kind=inner Drops on $left.DvcHostname == $right.DvcHostname
| where TimeGenerated between (DropTime .. DropTime + 30m)
| project DropTime, ExecTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          DroppedFile = TargetFilePath, DropParent,
          ChildImage = TargetProcessName, ChildCmd = TargetProcessCommandLine
| order by ExecTime desc
ucs.rationale: Every major MuddyWater write-up since 2020 (CISA AA22-055A, Microsoft Mango Sandstorm 2023, Group-IB Seedworm 2022, Proofpoint TA450 2024) calls out the *exact same* loadout: a spearphish landing page that hands the victim a fully-signed RMM installer. Detecting the file drop AND the user-launched install from a browser/mailer parent within minutes is the cluster's narrowest behavioural fingerprin

ucs.title: [LLM] MuddyWater POWERSTATS / MuddyC3 mshta-to-PowerShell loader chain
ucs.description: Catches the second-stage execution pattern documented for MuddyWater's POWERSTATS, POWERSTATS-NG, MuddyC3 and POWGOOP loaders: a Maldoc / HTA dropper invokes mshta.exe (often against a hosted .hta or via inline VBScript) which spawns powershell.exe with base64/-w hidden/IEX cradles to fetch the next stage. ClearSky, Trend Micro Earth Vetala, and Talos POWGOOP reports all describe this exact chain.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmdline from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="mshta.exe" (Processes.process IN ("*http*","*vbscript:*","*javascript:*","*.hta*")) Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","explorer.exe","cmd.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename _time as mshta_time | join type=inner dest user [| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="mshta.exe" Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe") (Processes.process IN ("*-w hidden*","*-WindowStyle Hidden*","*-enc*","*EncodedCommand*","*-nop*","*FromBase64String*","*DownloadString*","*IEX*","*Invoke-Expression*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process Processes.process_name _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename _time as ps_time process as ps_cmd] | where ps_time>=mshta_time AND ps_time<=mshta_time+120 | table mshta_time ps_time dest user parent_process_name cmdline ps_cmd | sort - mshta_time
ucs.kql:
let MshtaParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","explorer.exe","cmd.exe"]);
let PsBadCmd = dynamic(["-w hidden","-windowstyle hidden","-enc","encodedcommand","-nop","-noprofile","frombase64string","downloadstring","downloaddata","iex","invoke-expression","invoke-restmethod"]);
let Mshta = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FileName =~ "mshta.exe"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (MshtaParents)
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","vbscript:","javascript:",".hta")
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | project MshtaTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              MshtaParent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              MshtaCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              MshtaPid = ProcessId;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "mshta.exe"
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe")
| where tolower(ProcessCommandLine) has_any (PsBadCmd)
| join kind=inner Mshta on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (MshtaTime .. MshtaTime + 2m)
| extend B64 = extract(@"(?i)(?:-(?:e(?:nc(?:odedcommand)?)?))\s+([A-Za-z0-9+/=]{20,})", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| extend Decoded = iif(isnotempty(B64), base64_decode_tostring(B64), "")
| project MshtaTime, ChildTime = Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          MshtaParent, MshtaCmd,
          ChildImage = FileName, ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          Decoded
| order by MshtaTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let MshtaParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","explorer.exe","cmd.exe"]);
let PsBadCmd = dynamic(["-w hidden","-windowstyle hidden","-enc","encodedcommand","-nop","-noprofile","frombase64string","downloadstring","downloaddata","iex","invoke-expression","invoke-restmethod"]);
let Mshta = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where NewProcessName endswith "\\mshta.exe"
    | where ParentProcessName has_any (MshtaParents)
    | where CommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","vbscript:","javascript:",".hta")
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | project MshtaTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              MshtaParent = ParentProcessName,
              MshtaCmd = CommandLine,
              MshtaPid = NewProcessId;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith "\\mshta.exe"
| where NewProcessName has_any ("\\powershell.exe","\\pwsh.exe","\\cmd.exe")
| where tolower(CommandLine) has_any (PsBadCmd)
| join kind=inner Mshta on Computer
| where TimeGenerated between (MshtaTime .. MshtaTime + 2m)
| extend B64 = extract(@"(?i)(?:-(?:e(?:nc(?:odedcommand)?)?))\s+([A-Za-z0-9+/=]{20,})", 1, CommandLine)
| extend Decoded = iif(isnotempty(B64), base64_decode_tostring(B64), "")
| project MshtaTime, ChildTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          MshtaParent, MshtaCmd,
          ChildImage = NewProcessName, ChildCmd = CommandLine, Decoded
| order by MshtaTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: MuddyWater Mshta to PowerShell Loader Chain
id: 7d3e8f1c-9c20-4b2a-aa12-mw-mshta-ps01
status: experimental
description: Detects mshta.exe spawned by Office/script-host parents executing a remote .hta or vb/jsscript URI, immediately followed by powershell.exe with hidden/encoded/IEX cradles - signature MuddyWater POWERSTATS/MuddyC3 chain.
author: SOC Detection Engineering
references:
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa22-055a
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2023/04/18/mercury-and-dev-1084-destructive-attack-on-hybrid-environment/
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: process_creation
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\mshta.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
  selection_cmd:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '-w hidden'
      - '-WindowStyle Hidden'
      - '-enc'
      - 'EncodedCommand'
      - 'FromBase64String'
      - 'DownloadString'
      - 'Invoke-Expression'
      - 'IEX'
      - 'Invoke-RestMethod'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child and selection_cmd
falsepositives:
  - Legacy admin scripts that wrap PowerShell behind HTA installers
level: high
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1218.005
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.g0069
ucs.rationale: MuddyWater's POWERSTATS / POWERSTATS-NG / MuddyC3 / POWGOOP families have leaned on mshta.exe -> PowerShell as their second-stage loader since at least 2017 (Trend Micro POWERSTATS, ClearSky 2018, Cisco Talos 2022 POWGOOP, Deep Instinct PhonyC2 2023). The narrow shape - Office/script-host parent of mshta loading a remote URI/HTA, then mshta itself spawning hidden+encoded PowerShell within 2 minute

_field_issues.message: `Za` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`MshtaTime` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`MshtaParent` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`MshtaCmd` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`Decoded` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents. Did you mean `DeviceId`?

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `Za` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.
`MshtaTime` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.
`MshtaParent` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.
`MshtaCmd` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.
